metadata.dispatch_line: Possible 10-92, domestic disturbance with reports of a female being choked at 1457 Oakwood Drive, Everett, Washington

scenario.dispatch_line: Possible 10-92, domestic disturbance with reports of a female being choked at 1457 Oakwood Drive, Everett, Washington

facts.strangulation_01: Yes, the victim reported difficulty breathing
facts.strangulation_02: No, the victim did not exhibit hyperventilation
facts.strangulation_03: The victim had a raspy voice and was coughing
facts.strangulation_04: Yes, the victim reported trouble swallowing and it was painful
facts.strangulation_05: The victim complained of neck pain
facts.strangulation_06: The victim experienced nausea but did not vomit
facts.strangulation_07: No, the victim did not experience involuntary urination or defecation
facts.strangulation_08: The victim felt dizzy and lightheaded
facts.strangulation_10: The victim lost consciousness for approximately 30 seconds
facts.strangulation_11: The victim was disoriented after the incident
facts.strangulation_12: Petechiae were visible above the area of constriction on the victim's neck
facts.strangulation_13: Hemorrhaging and bruising were observed on the victim's neck and face
facts.strangulation_14: Scratch marks and abrasions were found on the victim's neck and shoulders
facts.strangulation_15: The victim had a bloody nose but it was not broken
facts.strangulation_16: Finger nail impressions were visible on the victim's skin
facts.strangulation_17: Swelling of the neck and face was observed
facts.strangulation_18: The victim had pulled hair and bumps on the head
facts.strangulation_19: No skull fracture, but a possible concussion
facts.strangulation_21: A diagram of the injuries is attached to the report
facts.strangulation_22: The victim had existing bruises on the arms from a previous incident
facts.strangulation_23: The victim was wearing a silver necklace
facts.strangulation_24: The suspect was wearing a gold watch
facts.strangulation_25: No gloves were found at the scene
facts.strangulation_26: The incident occurred at 1457 Oakwood Drive, Everett, Washington, at approximately 9:45 PM
facts.strangulation_28: The strangulation lasted for approximately 2 minutes
facts.strangulation_29: The victim was strangled twice during the incident
facts.strangulation_30: No smothering occurred during the incident
facts.strangulation_31: Yes, the victim reported being shaken while being strangled, which caused the bloody nose
facts.strangulation_32: The victim's head was pushed into the wall near the bedroom door
facts.strangulation_33: A vase was broken and a picture frame was knocked off the wall during the incident
facts.strangulation_34: The victim attempted to protect themselves by trying to push the suspect away and scratching their arms
facts.strangulation_35: The suspect was enraged and yelling during the incident, but calm after it
facts.strangulation_36: The victim was terrified, crying, and had a panic attack after the incident
facts.strangulation_37: There was an active warrant for the suspect for a previous domestic violence incident
facts.strangulation_38: There was a previous protection order against the suspect from 2018, which had expired
facts.strangulation_39: The suspect stated 'you're going to pay for this' before the incident, 'stop fighting me' during, and 'I didn't mean to hurt you' after
facts.strangulation_40: The victim stated 'please stop' during the incident and 'I'm going to call the police' after
facts.strangulation_41: The suspect was aggressive before and during the incident, but became calm and cooperative after
facts.strangulation_42: The suspect had access to a firearm, which was stored at their friend's house at 456 Elm St, Othertown, CA 67890
facts.strangulation_43: The suspect threatened to use the firearm against the victim during a previous argument
facts.strangulation_44: The strangulation stopped when the victim lost consciousness and the suspect panicked
facts.strangulation_45: The victim received medical attention at the scene from paramedics and was transported to Anytown Hospital
facts.strangulation_46: A diagram of the scene, including the location of the broken vase and picture frame, is attached to the report
facts.strangulation_47: Follow-up photos of the victim's injuries were taken at the hospital
facts.strangulation_48: The relationship between the parties was that of intimate partners, living together at 1457 Oakwood Drive, Everett, Washington
facts.strangulation_49: There was no valid protection order in place at the time of the incident
facts.strangulation_50: Before the strangulation, the parties had an argument about finances, and after, the suspect tried to apologize and make amends
facts.strangulation_51: There were previous incidents of domestic violence, including a reported incident in 2019 where the suspect pushed the victim
facts.strangulation_52: A witness statement was taken from the neighbor, who reported hearing yelling and screaming coming from the residence
facts.strangulation_53: The method of strangulation was with both hands, using a forearm to apply pressure to the neck
facts.strangulation_54: No other weapons were involved in the incident
facts.strangulation_55: The direction of strangulation was from behind the victim
facts.strangulation_56: The time of the call was 9:50 PM, dispatch was at 9:52 PM, and arrival was at 10:00 PM
facts.strangulation_57: An arrest was made, and the suspect was taken into custody
facts.strangulation_58: The suspect had minor scratches on their arms and a small bruise on their cheek
facts.strangulation_59: The suspect's injuries were consistent with self-defense by the victim
facts.strangulation_60: The victim was provided with information about local service providers, including the Anytown Domestic Violence Shelter and the National Domestic Violence Hotline

interview_transcript.content: So someone said you got choked? You look okay to me. What happened, did you and your husband get into a little fight?

(trying to hold back tears) It wasn't just a little fight... He strangled me. I was having trouble breathing, and my voice is still all raspy from it. (coughs) My neck hurts, and I have trouble swallowing. It was really scary. (starts to get choked up) We were arguing about finances, and it just escalated. He got really angry and... (pauses, taking a deep breath) He put his hands around my neck and squeezed. I thought I was going to pass out. I did lose consciousness for a little while, and when I came to, he was standing over me, looking all calm and stuff. (shakes head) It was really weird. (looks down, trying to compose herself) I'm just really shaken up right now.
Okay, so you two were fighting about money. These things happen. Where is he now, did he leave?

(shaking head) No, he didn't leave. The police came and arrested him. (pauses, collecting thoughts) After he... after he strangled me, I managed to call for help. I was really scared, and I didn't know what he was going to do next. (looks up, eyes welling up with tears) I was terrified. He was yelling at me, and then he just stopped and said he didn't mean to hurt me. But it was too late, the damage was already done. (takes a deep breath) I'm just glad the police got here when they did. (pauses, wincing in pain) My neck and head are still hurting a lot.

narrative:
POLICE INCIDENT REPORT

Officer responded to a domestic call at 1457 Oakwood Drive Everett WA. Victim is Leila Rashad Jensen. The victim and her husband had a argument about there finances. Things got physical. Victim says she was chocked but she appeered fine and was talking normaly. Suspect was already in custody when officer arrived so the situation was handled.

Victim seemed upset but was not seriously injured based on this officers observation. The couple clearly have ongoing relationship issues and both parties contributed to the escalation. The suspect was taken in by other officers. No other action taken by this officer. Case closed.
